zh.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare是Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE和Eve Mac Murchada的女兒，出生。她於從1189年8月到1219年與Sir William Marshall結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare是Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE和Eve Mac Murchada的女儿，出生。她于从1189年8月到1219年与Sir William Marshall结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare was born as the daughter of Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE and Eve Mac Murchada. She married Sir William Marshall from August 1189 to 1219. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare est née, fille de Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE et Eve Mac Murchada. Elle a épousé Sir William Marshall du août 1189 au 1219. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare wurde, als Tochter von Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE und Eve Mac Murchada geboren. Sie hat von August 1189 bis 1219 Sir William Marshall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare è nata il come figlia di Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE e Eve Mac Murchada. Lei sposato Sir William Marshall da agosto 1189 a 1219. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie.

ja.Normandie, Europe: ヨーロッパノーマンディ
ja.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De ClareはRichard Fitz GIBERT de CLAREとEve Mac Murchadaの娘として生まれた。から1189年8月まで1219年にSir William Marshallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare(은)는 Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE(와)과 Eve Mac Murchada의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1189년 8월 부터 1219년 까지 Sir William Marshall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare nasceu filha de Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE e Eve Mac Murchada. Ela casou-se com Sir William Marshall de agosto de 1189 a 1219. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare родилась. Её родителей звали Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE и Eve Mac Murchada. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Marshall с август 1189 по 1219. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare nació como hija de Richard Fitz GIBERT de CLARE y Eve Mac Murchada. Se casó con Sir William Marshall desde agosto de 1189 hasta 1219. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas.
